2018-04-25.abstract: The speed and unpredictability of the summit-level diplomacy has left Tokyo unsettled at everything from the geopolitics of talks to the dessert menu.

2018-04-26.title: The Korean War in Pictures
2018-04-26.abstract: Three years of fighting ended when North Korea, China and the United States reached an armistice in 1953. But no formal peace treaty was ever brokered.

2018-04-26.title: When Modi and Xi Meet, Indian Elections Will Set the Tone
2018-04-26.abstract: At a meeting in China, Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India will, explicitly or not, be seeking President Xi Jinping’s help to win next year’s Indian election.

2018-04-26.title: Nicaragua’s Sudden Uprising
2018-04-26.abstract: Deadly protests set off by unpopular changes to social security in Nicaragua show no signs of abating, even after the president gave in to demands.

2018-04-26.title: In Just a Week, ‘Nicaragua Changed’ as Protesters Cracked a Leader’s Grip
2018-04-26.abstract: President Daniel Ortega, once a revolutionary hero, faces Nicaragua’s biggest uprising in decades as protesters pry themselves free from his control.

2018-04-26.title: Philippine Envoy to Kuwait Expelled After So-Called Rescue of Domestic Workers
2018-04-26.abstract: The expulsion was the latest action in a mounting diplomatic dispute prompted by the discovery of a Filipina maid’s body in a freezer in February.

2018-04-27.title: Kim Jong-un Crosses Inter-Korean Border
2018-04-27.abstract: North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, walked across the inter-Korean border to meet the South’s president, Moon Jae-in. No North Korean leader had made the journey in the two countries’ 70-year history of bitter rivalry.

2018-04-27.title: North and South Korea Summit Is Short on Details, but Long on Theater
2018-04-27.abstract: It was the stagecraft of Friday’s meeting that offered hope, when statecraft delivered only vague pledges to work toward a peace treaty.

2018-04-27.title: North and South Korea Set Bold Goals: A Final Peace and No Nuclear Arms
2018-04-27.abstract: Kim Jong-un of North Korea and Moon Jae-in of the South agreed to work to formally end the Korean War and rid the peninsula of nuclear weapons.

2018-04-27.title: Mike Pompeo, Wasting No Time, Warns Europe About Iran Deal
2018-04-27.abstract: A day after being sworn in, the top American diplomat told leaders of the NATO alliance in Brussels that President Trump might soon scrap the Iran nuclear deal.

2018-04-27.title: Louis Arthur Charles: The British Royal Baby’s Name Is Revealed
2018-04-27.abstract: Prince Louis, the third child of Prince William and his wife, Catherine, is the fifth in line to the British throne.

2018-04-28.title: How Trump’s Mixed Signals Complicate America’s Role in the World
2018-04-28.abstract: President Trump’s inconsistent responses to Iran and North Korea risk conveying a very different message to the world than the one he may wish to send.

2018-04-28.title: Alfie Evans, Terminally Ill British Toddler at Center of Court Fight, Dies
2018-04-28.abstract: The 23-month-old had a rare degenerative brain condition. His parents fought to continue treatment, but his doctors said further medical care would have been futile.

2018-04-28.title: Irish Voters Speak Out on Landmark Abortion Referendum
2018-04-28.abstract: The Times asked readers in Ireland to give their opinion about the country’s coming vote on lifting the constitutional ban on abortion.

2018-04-28.title: ‘Their Country Is Being Invaded’: Exodus of Venezuelans Overwhelms Northern Brazil
2018-04-28.abstract: As thousands of Venezuelans flee their homeland each day, the region’s tolerance for immigrants is being tested.

2018-04-28.title: North Korean News Outlets Fawn Over Kim but Offer Few Clues on Nuclear Plan
2018-04-28.abstract: After the inter-Korean summit meeting, the crucial question remains: Is Kim Jong-un willing to abandon his atomic weapons?

2018-04-29.title: What May Life in Italy Be Like Under the Right? These Immigrants Already Know
2018-04-29.abstract: On the outskirts of Milan, one town’s new mayor has blocked construction of a mosque, cut services to migrants and given priority to “Italians first.”

2018-04-29.title: To Build Dubai of the Balkans, Serbia Deploys Bulldozers and Baseball Bats
2018-04-29.abstract: The government’s plan to make Belgrade a playground for the rich involves foreign money and evicting residents with what critics call strong-arm tactics.

2018-04-29.title: Kim Says He’d End North Korea Nuclear Pursuit for U.S. Truce
2018-04-29.abstract: South Korean officials said the North Korean leader, Kim Jong-un, had told them he would abandon his nuclear weapons if Washington ended the Korean War and promised nonaggression.

2018-04-29.title: Australia Pledges Millions of Dollars in Bid to Rescue Great Barrier Reef
2018-04-29.abstract: The government will set aside 500 million Australian dollars to help the global treasure after years of damage from warming waters caused by climate change.

2018-04-29.title: He Drove a Tesla on Autopilot From the Passenger Seat. The Court Was Not Amused.
2018-04-29.abstract: The British man was barred from driving for 18 months after being videotaped sitting with his hands behind his head, cruising at 40 miles per hour in “heavy” traffic.

2018-04-30.title: At Toronto Vigil After Van Attack: Sadness, Civic Pride and Unease
2018-04-30.abstract: Thousands marched to remember the 10 people killed. While many were proud of the city’s response, others worried about what had motivated the attack.

2018-04-30.title: Sajid Javid Is Named U.K. Home Secretary, Replacing Amber Rudd
2018-04-30.abstract: The new minister, a son of Pakistani immigrants, will have the immediate task of quieting the ruckus over the “Windrush generation.”

2018-04-30.title: South Korea to End Propaganda Broadcasts Along Border With North
2018-04-30.abstract: As part of a summit agreement, the South said it would be removing propaganda loudspeakers and the North decreed it would synchronize its clocks with the South.

2018-04-30.title: Journalists Suffer Deadliest Day in Afghanistan Since at Least 2002
2018-04-30.abstract: A blast on a street full of state and diplomatic offices was followed by another 40 minutes later; at least 25 were killed, including nine journalists.

2018-04-30.title: Missile Attack in Syria Reportedly Kills at Least 16, Raising Regional Tensions
2018-04-30.abstract: Suspicions fell on Israel, which has bombed what are believed to be convoys transporting Iran’s weapons to its regional allies.
